title: Broadcast Operations Control Center
subtitle: Real-time operational visibility for live events

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: The Broadcast Operations Control Center is mission control for live streaming. Just as NASA monitors every system on a spacecraft from one room, BOCC gives operators a single pane of glass over every stream, every encoder, every CDN edge — during the most high-pressure moments in media.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
BOCC provides real-time operational dashboards for live broadcasts:

- **Stream health monitoring**: Ingest status, encoder output quality, manifest availability
- **CDN status**: Per-PoP cache health, error rates, throughput by region
- **Viewer impact**: Real-time concurrent viewer count correlated with quality metrics
- **Alerting**: Threshold-based alerts for encoder failures, CDN errors, and QoE degradation
- **Multi-CDN visibility**: When using multiple CDNs, compare performance side-by-side

Integrates with MSL, AMD, and third-party monitoring tools (MediaMelon, Conviva).

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: FIFA World Cup final: 20M+ concurrent viewers across 200 countries. The BOCC team monitors encoder health, CDN throughput per region, and viewer-side QoE — all in real time. If a PoP degrades, traffic reroutes automatically via GTM; BOCC confirms the shift and validates viewer experience recovery within seconds.
"BOCC = mission control for live broadcasting. Single pane of glass over encoders, CDN, and viewer QoE during high-pressure live events."

quiz.question: During a World Cup match, a PoP in South America shows elevated error rates. What does the BOCC team see?

options.text: Nothing — they find out from Twitter complaints
options.explain: BOCC provides real-time alerting. They know before viewers complain.

options.text: Real-time alerts, per-PoP error rates, and automatic GTM rerouting — they validate recovery within seconds
options.explain: BOCC surfaces the issue, GTM reroutes traffic, and operators confirm viewer QoE normalizes.
Live events need real-time monitoring, not weekly reports.

options.text: They have to manually check each PoP
options.explain: BOCC aggregates all PoPs into a single dashboard with automated alerting.

quiz.question: A broadcaster uses Akamai and a backup CDN. How does BOCC help?

options.text: Multi-CDN comparison: side-by-side performance, error rates, and viewer QoE across all CDN providers
options.explain: Operators see which CDN is performing better per region and can shift traffic accordingly.

options.text: It automatically switches all traffic to the cheapest CDN
options.explain: Traffic decisions are based on quality, not cost. Operators make the call.
